title: Fresh Cranberry Salsa with Cream Cheese
description: Bright, tart cranberry salsa with jalapeño, green onion, and cilantro, spooned over cream cheese and served with crackers. A crowd-pleasing appetizer that comes together fast.

ingredients.name: green onions, sliced and roughly chopped

instructions.text: Place cranberries, jalapeños, green onions, and cilantro in a food processor and pulse until finely chopped.
Transfer to a bowl and stir in lime juice, salt, and sugar. Taste and adjust sweetness as needed. Refrigerate for at least 2 hours to let the flavors develop.
Spread one block of cream cheese evenly in a pie plate or shallow serving dish. Spoon salsa over the top. Serve with crackers.

notes:
One batch of salsa is enough for both blocks of cream cheese. Serve over one block at a time and refrigerate the remaining salsa for up to a few days.

Adjust freely: more onion and cilantro add brightness; more jalapeño adds heat. The sugar level is also a matter of taste — start with half a cup and add from there.

Gluten-free as written; just pair with GF crackers to keep it that way.
